paper_title: Phytosociological and ecological discrimination of Mediterranean cypress ('Cupressus sempervirens') communities in Crete (Greece) by means of pollen analysis

methods.description: Process for collecting vegetation and pollen samples across various locations in Crete to analyze modern pollen rain and plant communities dominated by Cupressus sempervirens.

steps.description: Vegetation and pollen samples were collected in 2017 at 60 locations on Crete.
steps.searchable_summary: collect vegetation and pollen samples from 60 locations on Crete

steps.description: Moss polsters samples were collected at each location to provide modern pollen data, with positional and altitudinal data recorded using a portable Juno 3D Trimble Ltd. GPS device.
steps.searchable_summary: collect moss polsters samples and record positional and altitudinal data using GPS

steps.description: Moss samples were collected over an area of approximately 100 m² by taking multiple moss polsters from the concerned site to ensure an even representation.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and combine multiple moss polsters from 100 m² area

steps.description: A relevé of vegetation was also made at each sampling site following the physotociological approach.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct vegetation relevé at each sampling site

steps.description: Ten environmental variables were available for 60 sites, including bioclimatic variables from the WorldClim database.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch environmental variables from WorldClim database

methods.description: Laboratory procedures for processing moss polsters samples to identify and count pollen, spores, and non-pollen palynomorphs.

steps.description: Moss polsters samples of approximately 10 cm³ were homogenized prior to extraction.
The samples were sieved through 1 mm screens to remove larger particles.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve samples to remove larger particles

steps.description: Samples were processed following the standard protocol developed by Faegri & Iversen (1989).
steps.searchable_summary: process samples using Faegri & Iversen protocol

steps.description: Samples were stored in glycerol, mounted on microscope slides and examined with a Nikon Eclipse 50i light-microscope to identify pollen, spores and non-pollen palynomorphs.
steps.searchable_summary: examine samples under microscope to identify pollen and spores

steps.description: Pollen grains, spores and non-pollen palynomorphs were identified according to Moore et al. (1991) and López-Sáez & López-Merino (2007).
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen grains using reference literature

steps.description: A minimum of 300 pollen grains were identified and counted for each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: count and record at least 300 pollen grains per sample

steps.description: Pollen percentages were calculated and presented as bars in a pollen diagram using Tilia and TGView software.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pollen percentages and plot in diagram
